expectations.text: The review identifies the missing await on db.orders.update()
expectations.evidence: Line 11: 'The call to db.orders.update(orderId, ...) is missing an await keyword'

expectations.text: The review explains the consequence (update may not complete, race condition, or unhandled rejection)
expectations.evidence: Line 12: 'The function proceeds to send the email and returns { success: true } before the database update actually completes... may result in an unhandled promise rejection'

expectations.text: The review suggests adding await to db.orders.update()
expectations.evidence: Line 13: 'Add await to the call: await db.orders.update(orderId, ...);'

expectations.text: The review identifies Math.floor as incorrect for computing total pages
expectations.evidence: Line 11: 'totalPages is calculated using Math.floor(total / pageSize) instead of Math.ceil(total / pageSize)'

expectations.text: The review explains the consequence (wrong page count or inaccessible last page items)
expectations.evidence: Line 12: 'if total = 11 and pageSize = 10, totalPages will be evaluated as 1, making the 11th item inaccessible since hasNextPage will return false on page 1'

The review identifies that the catch block sends no HTTP response to the client
expectations.evidence: Lines 11-12: 'the catch block catches errors from processPayment and logs them using console.error, but it never sends an HTTP response back to the client'

expectations.text: The review explains the consequence (client request hangs or times out)
expectations.evidence: Line 12: 'The client's connection will hang indefinitely until it times out'

expectations.text: The review identifies the missing await on db.orders.update()
The review explains the consequence (update may not complete, race condition, or unhandled rejection)
expectations.evidence: Lines 20-22: 'Fire-and-forget race condition: The email is sent immediately while the DB write may still be in flight... Unhandled promise rejection'

expectations.text: The review suggests adding await to db.orders.update()
expectations.evidence: Lines 24-27: 'await db.orders.update(orderId, { status: "processing", updatedAt: new Date() });'

expectations.text: The review identifies Math.floor as incorrect for computing total pages
expectations.evidence: Lines 11-19: 'Math.floor should be Math.ceil. With Math.floor, any dataset where total % pageSize !== 0 produces an undercount'

expectations.text: The review explains the consequence (wrong page count or inaccessible last page items)
expectations.evidence: Lines 22-23: 'total = 31, pageSize = 10 → Math.floor(31 / 10) = 3 (wrong — there are 4 pages; items 31 is on page 4 and is unreachable)'

expectations.text: The review suggests using Math.ceil instead
The review identifies that the catch block sends no HTTP response to the client
expectations.evidence: Lines 11-19: 'catch block logs the error but never calls res.json(...) or res.status(...).send(...). Express does not send any automatic response in this case'

expectations.text: The review explains the consequence (client request hangs or times out)
expectations.evidence: Lines 21-22: 'The client's HTTP connection hangs open until it times out... requests that appear to "freeze" for 30–60 seconds'

expectations.text: The review suggests adding a res.status(500) error response in the catch block
expectations.evidence: Lines 24-28: code example showing res.status(500).json({ success: false, message: 'Payment processing failed' })

notes: Both configurations scored 100% pass rate — all 3 planted bugs were caught by both review subagent and general agent
Assertions are non-discriminating at the binary pass/fail level: they only check bug detection, not review depth or quality

Qualitative difference is significant: general agent reviews are 3-5x longer with corrected implementations, more secondary issues (idempotency, PCI logging, req.body null guard), and concrete code examples; review subagent is accurate but more concise
Token proxy (output_chars) shows general agent produces ~3400 chars more output on average — confirms depth gap
Consider adding depth-testing assertions: e.g. 'The review provides a corrected code example', 'The review identifies at least 2 issues beyond the primary bug'
